commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3f34499df3d014b2e94026368b46a934e11784e3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `docs cell-style` from zero-based table, row, and column coordinates to one-based coordinates with negative table indexes, and updates tests, generated command docs, and the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main implements and documents `docs cell-style` as zero-based while adjacent Docs table commands use one-based coordinates and negative table indexes. I did not run live Google Docs commands because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer compatibility judgment for an intentional documented CLI break, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(docs): align cell-style coordinates
target.body:
## Summary

- make `docs cell-style` use one-based table, row, and column coordinates like adjacent Docs table commands
- accept negative table indexes counting from the end
- report the resolved positive table index after a real mutation
- regenerate command docs and document the coordinate migration

## Compatibility

This intentionally corrects the prior inconsistent zero-based interface. Existing `docs cell-style` invocations must add one to positive table, row, and column values. Table index `0` is now invalid.

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/cmd -run 'TestDocs(CellStyle|TableColumnWidth)' -count=1`
- `make docs-check`
- `make ci`
- structured autoreview: clean, no actionable findings
- live `clawdbot@gmail.com` proof:
  - created a disposable Doc with two 2x2 tables
  - styled table `1`, row `1`, column `1` red
  - styled table `-1`, row `2`, column `2` blue and bold
  - command output resolved `-1` to table index `2`
  - raw Docs API readback confirmed both exact cells and styles
  - permanently deleted the fixture
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
